items.title: NT government extends town camp alcohol bans

children.content: The NT government is extending alcohol bans in town camps beyond 2027, in a move welcomed by the health sector as a win for community safety.

gallery.altText: A row of empty liquor bottles lined up on a kitchen bench, the adjoining walls are covered in marks and dents.

items.title: NT's deputy opposition leader dumped for new recruit

children.content: The Northern Territory Labor party has replaced its deputy opposition leader with a newcomer to parliament, in a move they say will win back the trust and confidence of the people of Darwin.

image.altText: A man wearing a red shirt standing in a shed, looking directly down the camera.

items.title: Call for youth foyer to help tackle homelessness in the NT

children.content: Youth homelessness rates remain stubbornly high in Central Australia and advocates are renewing calls for a transitional housing model for young people to be established to tackle the issue.

image.altText: a pile of pillows on a sandy ground with bushland in the background

items.title: In pictures: Alice Springs celebrates 30 years of unique craft festival

children.content: As the Alice Springs Beanie Festival celebrates its 30th and final year, we're exploring the weird and wonderful handmade creations — and the characters working hard behind the scenes — that have made the annual event so special.

A group of women wearing colourful knit beanies, smiling as they take a photo together.
A boy holds a colourful knitted fish while wearing a handmade beanie, which has small yellow creature stitched on the top.

items.title: Couple forced to live on verandah for three months awaiting flood repairs

children.content: Katherine couple Nicki and Luke Asling have been living on their back verandah for the past three months, after their home was flooded to the ceiling in March. Local renovators say it will take months for all the repairs in town to be completed.

image.altText: A woman sits on a bed outdoors, patting a dog.

items.title: NT government reveals scale of bungled smart meter rollout

children.content: The Northern Territory's main energy retailer, Jacana, reveals about 5,000 customers — thousands more than previously thought — have been caught up in a smart meter bungle leading to long delays in their billing.

image.altText: A smart meter

items.title: How Ukraine is breaking through defences to bring war home to Moscow

children.content: Ukraine's growing arsenal of locally produced long-range drones are getting increasingly sophisticated, allowing Kyiv to increasingly strike targets across Moscow.

items.title: British 'King of the North' wins Westminster seat, poised for leadership challenge

children.content: Andy Burnham, a British Labour lifer, is expected to challenge Sir Keir Starmer for the country's top job after re-entering the House of Commons via a by-election.

items.title: Wife of AFL legend Tony Modra thanks first responders 'who saved his life'

children.content: The wife of AFL icon Tony Modra has expressed her gratitude to emergency services and hospital staff as the Crows legend remains in hospital in a "critical but stable condition" following a truck crash in South Australia.

items.title: 'Blue-sky thinking': North Queensland reacts to Rinehart's idea of giving islands to Musk

items.title: Pauline Hanson says businesses 'can't sack' lazy workers. What's the reality?

children.content: Pauline Hanson's claims that Australian businesses are "tied" to lazy workers they "can't sack", are labelled inaccurate by some employment law experts and small business advocates, while one HR representative says businesses are "terrified to terminate".

items.title: Alleged 'black flight' pilot arrested at Brisbane Airport

items.title: So-called ISIS bride believes terrorist group 'sucks', lawyer says

items.title: WA government wants federal help with Collie's coal transition

items.title: Fluke, or are the Socceroos actually good enough to win against the US?

Was the Socceroos' win against Türkiye a fluke or was the way the game went part of coach Tony Popovic's plan?
We take a closer look at whether Australia is being judged on the wrong metrics and why this team has fans excited.

items.title: 'Not about mass surveillance': WA Police first in country to use live facial recognition

items.title: Superbikes move leaves Phillip Island community in limbo

items.title: Waterloo demolition to commence after activist camp cleared out

items.title: Obama centre opens with star-studded crowd but no Trump

items.title: Auslan news service run by Deaf journalists renewed for another year
